title: Add canary A/B validation for ANALYZE phase skill edits before applying
description: Currently, the ANALYZE phase applies skill file changes immediately after the Opus analyzer proposes them. If a change degrades agent quality, this is only detected on the NEXT run (24-hour feedback delay) via prompt_tracker regression data. Meta's HyperAgents research demonstrates a 'sandboxed testing' pattern where candidate changes are validated before deployment using canary evaluation. Apply this pattern to MindPattern: when ANALYZE proposes a skill file edit, run the affected agent twice on a sample of today's preflight data — once with the current skill file, once with the proposed edit — then compare finding quality scores. Only apply the edit if the new skill produces equal or better quality. This closes the 24-hour feedback gap and prevents bad edits from persisting for an entire run cycle.
requirements: Add canary_eval_skill_change(agent_name, old_skill_content, new_skill_content, sample_preflight) function in analyzer.py
Sample 5-8 preflight items from today's data for the affected agent as canary input
Run run_single_agent() twice with identical prompt except for skill file content (old vs new)
Compare outputs using grade_finding() from R018 (or simpler: count of valid findings + average summary length as fallback)
Only apply the ANALYZE edit if new_score >= old_score * 0.95 (allow 5% tolerance for variance)
Log canary results (old_score, new_score, decision) to traces.db
Skip canary for append-only changes (low risk) — only canary for replace actions
Add --skip-canary flag to allow bypassing for manual runs
done_criteria: ANALYZE edits with action=replace go through canary validation before applying
Canary results logged in traces.db with old_score, new_score, and apply/reject decision
Bad edits (new_score < 0.95 * old_score) are rejected with logged reason
Append-only edits skip canary and apply directly
Pipeline run time increases by <5 minutes (canary runs use Haiku, not Sonnet)

design_spec.architecture_impact: Adds a validation step between ANALYZE output parsing and apply_analyzer_changes(). The canary uses a lightweight agent run (Haiku model, max 5 turns, 60s timeout) to keep cost low. This is architecturally similar to HyperAgents' sandboxed testing — the improvement engine stays fixed while the changes are validated.

context: Meta's HyperAgents research shows self-improving AI systems need 'sandboxed testing' and 'canary deployments' before applying self-modifications. MindPattern's ANALYZE phase currently has a 24-hour feedback delay for regression detection. This ticket adds same-run validation. Source: https://pooya.blog/blog/hyperagents-self-improving-ai-meta-research-2026/ and ICLR 2026 Workshop on Recursive Self-Improvement: https://recursive-workshop.github.io/
